name: whois
title: whois
description:
intelligent WHOIS client
This package provides a commandline client for the WHOIS (RFC 3912)
protocol, which queries online servers for information such as contact
details for domains and IP address assignments.
It can intelligently select the appropriate WHOIS server for most queries.
.
The package also contains mkpasswd, a features-rich front end to the
password encryption function crypt(3).

commands.name: whois
commands.description: client for the whois directory service

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use mkpasswd because intelligent WHOIS client.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use mkpasswd as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `mkpasswd=$KFIOSA_SECRET [SALT]` as documented upstream for mkpasswd.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair mkpasswd with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `mkpasswd --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: mkpasswd - Overfeatured front end to crypt(3)
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install whois. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `mkpasswd=$KFIOSA_SECRET [SALT]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: intelligent WHOIS client.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: mkpasswd - Overfeatured front end to crypt(3).
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install whois
